title: Clean Fuels Weekly Commentary: LCFS Credit Prices Maintain Upward Trend; Type 1 Trades Fall WoW
content:
This Weekâ€™s Highlights

(19th January 2026 â€“ 25th January 2026)
  • A total of 732,048 credits were exchanged as Type 1 trades, a decrease of 18.51% week-over-week (WoW), while all non-zero trades increased 8.90% WoW and a total of 1,994,493 non-zero credits were traded in CA LCFS.
  • The weekly average CA LCFS credit price stood at $58.79/MT for Type 1 transfers, up by 0.86% WoW; prices of all non-zero transfers rose by 6.05%, price stood at $62.77/MT.
  • The average LCFS spot credit price rose by 4.43% WoW; on 27th January, the spot price stood at $63.00/MT, and the benchmark price stood at $66.38/MT.
  • D3 RIN prices fell by 0.41% WoW, while D4, D5 and D6 RIN prices increased by 6.90%, 6.50% and 7.08% WoW respectively.

cCarbonâ€™s Market View

California Energy Commission (CEC) announced that California surpassed 2.5 million cumulative new ZEV sales in 2025, marking over 300% growth since 2019, driven by strong state policies and incentives. State officials highlighted Californiaâ€™s unmatched five-year growth in ZEV adoption, sustained consumer demand, and major investments in charging infrastructure.

CA LCFS Weekly Price and Volume Trend

[caption id="attachment_611865" align="alignnone" width="709"] Table 1. Weekly average credit prices and weekly volumes, Source: California ARB[/caption] The average Type 1 transfer price increased by 0.86% WoW, rising from $58.29 to $58.79, while all non-zero transfer prices moved up from $59.19 to $62.77, a 6.05% WoW gain. Type 1 trades totaled 732,048 credits, reflecting a 18.51% WoW decrease and total non-zero transaction volumes rose by 8.90% WoW, reaching 1,994,493 credits for the week. [caption id="attachment_611866" align="alignnone" width="1300"] Fig 1. Weekly average credit prices and weekly volumes, Source: California ARB[/caption]

CA LCFS Daily Average Spot Price

[caption id="attachment_611867" align="alignnone" width="1300"] Fig 2. California LCFS Weekly average spot prices and benchmark (*CC.Info OTC: Numbers are an average of broker prices reported to CC.info)[/caption] Last week, the average LCFS spot credit prices rose by 4.43% WoW, reaching $62.03/MT. On 27th January, the spot price stood at $63.00/MT. The benchmark price, representing the future price of contracts expiring at the end of 2026, stood at $66.38/MT on 27th January.

LCFS Weekly Trade Frequency and Volume

[caption id="attachment_611868" align="alignnone" width="1300"] Fig 3. Trade frequency and volume, Source: California ARB[/caption] As per the data reported by CARB last week, a total of 115 trades took place in the market, including 11 high-volume transactions. The highest reported transfer price was $77.35 for a Type 2 transaction involving 500,000 credits. The lowest transfer price was $51.00 for a Type 2 transaction covering 5,000 credits. The largest trade by volume was a Type 2 transaction of 500,000 credits priced at $77.35, while the smallest was a Type 1 transfer of 39 credits priced at $59.50.

CA LCFS Monthly Volumes and Price Trend

[caption id="attachment_611870" align="alignnone" width="1300"] Fig 4. Total monthly volumes and associated prices, Source: California ARB[/caption] The average Type 1 spot price stood at $57.94 in January so far, rising from $53.08 in December. As of 24th January 2026, trade volumes for the month reached 6.53M.

CA LCFS Weekly Volume Price Trend

[caption id="attachment_611871" align="alignnone" width="1300"] Fig 5. Volume Price Trend indicator for the CA LCFS market, Source: California ARB[/caption] The Volume Price Trend Indicator (VPT) helps identify the parity between supply and demand for stock. The idea behind the indicator is to multiply the market volume of LCFS credits by the percentage change in its price. If the credit price declines, the value of the indicator falls. Across 19th January 2026 â€“ 25th January 2026, total VPT rose by 9.04% WoW, with a rise in all non-zero credit prices for the last week.

Renewable Fuel Standard (RFS): RIN Weekly Price Trend

RINs are credits used for compliance and are the â€œcurrencyâ€ of the RFS program. Renewable fuel producers generate RINs, market participants trade RINs, and obligated parties obtain and then ultimately retire RINs for the compliance list. D3 RIN prices fell by 0.41% WoW, while D4, D5 and D6 RIN prices increased by 6.90%, 6.50% and 7.08% WoW respectively. D3: Cellulosic biofuel D4: Biomass-based diesel D5: Advanced biofuels (produced from any type of renewable biomass except corn starch ethanol) D6: Conventional renewable biofuel ethanol derived from corn starch, or any other qualifying renewable fuel

Upcoming DatesÂ 

Key Clean Fuels Market Developments This Week

  • Emissions Reduction Alberta Launches Fuel Innovation Fund as a Compliance Option Under Canadaâ€™s Clean Fuel Regulations: Emissions Reduction Alberta (ERA) has been approved as an Emission Reduction Funding Program under Canadaâ€™s Clean Fuel Regulations. Its new subsidiary, the Fuel Innovation Fund, will provide regulated fuel suppliers, industry innovators, and technology developers with an opportunity to deploy technology that lowers the carbon intensity of transportation fuels.The Fuel Innovation Fund has two funding streams: a continuous intake program where primary suppliers can apply to reinvest into their own capital projects and an annual competitive funding that will support a broad range of applicants.
  • New Mexico Officially Adopts First Clean Fuel Market in the Southwest: The New Mexico Environmental Improvement Board today unanimously adopted regulations establishing the Clean Transportation Fuel Program, setting April 1 as the opening date for the Southwest's first clean fuel market. Signed into law by Gov. Michelle Lujan Grisham in 2024, the Clean Transportation Fuel Program is a market-based initiative that incentivizes and expands access to lower-carbon transportation fuels, including ethanol, biofuels, renewable diesel, renewable natural gas, electricity, and supporting infrastructure.
  • Rule Announcement Notice: Clean Fuel Standard Updates in Washington: The Washington State Department of Ecology has initiated a new rulemaking process to update the Clean Fuel Standard, implementing Second Substitute House Bill 1409 which was signed into law in May 2025. This update amends Chapter 173-424 WAC and associated fee structures to aggressively decarbonize the transportation sector, the state's largest source of greenhouse gas emissions. The new rules significantly increase carbon intensity reduction targets: previously set at a 20% reduction by 2038, the standard now mandates a 45% reduction below 2017 levels by 2038, with a provision to reach 55% if specific conditions are met.
  • California Surpasses 2.5 Million ZEV Sales: California Energy Commission (CEC) announced that California surpassed 2.5 million cumulative new zero-emission vehicle (ZEV) sales in 2025. Since the end of 2019, cumulative new ZEV sales in California alone have grown over 300%, driven by effective clean transportation policy and various ZEV incentives. This remarkable growth has also driven the stateâ€™s commitment to building a robust ZEV infrastructure network. â€œNo state in the union can match Californiaâ€™s five-year growth in ZEV sales,â€ said California Energy Commissioner Nancy Skinner.
  • ECCCâ€™s Low Carbon Fuels Division Releases Registered Emission-Reduction Funding Program Credit Price and Compliance Credit Clearance Mechanism: In respect of the 2025 compliance period, the emission-reduction funding program credit price is $380 CAD, calculated in accordance with subsection 118(4) of the Regulations. Primary suppliers may use compliance credits created from a contribution made to a registered emission-reduction funding programs to satisfy up to 10% of their total reduction requirement. A list of registered emission-reduction funding programs is now available on the CFR Webpage: Compliance with the Clean Fuel Regulations. In respect of the 2025 compliance period, the maximum price of a compliance credit transferred through the compliance-credit clearance mechanism is $326 CAD, calculated in accordance with subsection 112(3) of the Regulations.
  • Clean Fuels Urges President Trump to Quickly Finalize RFS, 45Z Rules: Clean Fuels Alliance America sent a letter to President Donald Trump highlighting the biodiesel, renewable diesel and SAF industryâ€™s contribution to U.S. energy security and rural prosperity, as the President embarks on visits across the country to highlight economic conditions. The letter emphasizes that the industryâ€™s continued growth is at risk as farmers and fuel producers await regulatory rules â€“ specifically, the Renewable Fuel Standards for 2026 and 2027 and rules for the Section 45Z Clean Fuel Production Credit â€“ that were due under the prior administration.
